content: Just as language learning classrooms explicitly teach 'reflection' as a metacognitive skill to reinforce vocabulary retention and correct error patterns, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate structured, graded reflection scaffolding modeled after high school language learning curricula, rather than relying on unstructured free-form self-evaluation. This structured scaffolding would move beyond current multi-perspective reflection frameworks by introducing incremental reflection exercises that build an LLM’s ability to spot errors starting from simple output inconsistencies up to complex logical flaws.
